preview_text:
The model favours France at 41%, a modest edge over England's 28% win probability. The gap between the two reflects statistical expectation rather than a strong consensus; France holds roughly a 13-point advantage, leaving England a realistic path to victory on a neutral ground.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome in the model's view, at 31%, nearly matching England's win probability. This suggests the match shape leans toward competitive rather than one-sided—neither team is projected to dominate decisively, and a stalemate ranks as plausible as an England upset.

Model and market diverge noticeably on France's chances. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both price France substantially higher, near 50%, creating a 9-point spread between the model and consensus forecasts. The market also compresses the draw probability to around 25%, roughly 6 points below the model. The dashboard does not claim to resolve which view is correct; the divergence merits note when assessing odds.
